name: Andra Mari Eleiza and Path of the Three Crosses Loop
name_original: Andra Mari Eleiza y Camino de las Tres Cruces — circular desde Erandio
description: A moderate 6.4-mile jogging route in Erandio, offering estuary views and passing the Church of the Assumption and Path of the Three Crosses.
region_intro: Jogging around Erandio offers diverse landscapes, blending urban accessibility with natural beauty. The municipality is situated on the banks of the Bilbao Estuary, with the Nervión River providing scenic riverwalks. The terrain features gentle hills like San Pablo and San Bernabé, alongside more challenging ascents to summits such as Monte Karabezu. Green spaces and parks are integrated throughout the area, providing varied environments for running. Best jogging routes around Erandio The most popular jogging route is Andra Mari Eleiza and Path of the Three Crosses Loop, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines cultural landmarks with natural paths. Another top favourite among local runners is Edificio del Tigre – Pedro Arrupe Pedestrian Bridge loop from Erandio, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This route offers scenic views along the Bilbao Estuary. Local runners also love the Karabezu Summit – View from Monte Karabezu loop from Astrabudua, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Jogging around Erandio is defined by riverwalks along the Nervión, accessible urban parks, and ascents to local hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging summit trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Erandio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Erandio?
A: Erandio offers a diverse selection of over 15 running routes, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These routes range from easy riverside paths to more challenging ascents into the hills.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed run?
A: Yes, Erandio has several easy routes. The riverwalks along the Nervión River and the Bilbao Estuary provide flat, scenic paths perfect for a relaxed run. While specific easy routes aren't detailed in the top picks, the general terrain along the estuary is very accessible.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Erandio's running trails?
A: Erandio's running trails offer a varied terrain. You can expect flat paths along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/569708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbao Estuary&lt;/a&gt; and Nervión River, as well as more undulating paths through green spaces. For those seeking a challenge, routes ascend into the hills of San Pablo and San Bernabé, with some leading to summits like Monte Karabezu, featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Erandio?
A: Yes, many of Erandio's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1125368578/andra-mari-eleiza-and-path-of-the-three-crosses-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andra Mari Eleiza and Path of the Three Crosses Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40363059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Edificio del Tigre – Pedro Arrupe Pedestrian Bridge loop from Erandio&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views along the estuary.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in Erandio?
A: Absolutely. For runners looking for a challenge, Erandio's proximity to hills and mountains provides excellent options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31952859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Karabezu Summit – View from Monte Karabezu loop from Astrabudua&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.1-mile trail with significant elevation gain, perfect for experienced runners. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31915186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Karabezu Summit – Unbe Summit loop from Erandio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are Erandio's running routes accessible by public transport?
A: Erandio is well-connected by public transport, including the metro, making many running routes easily accessible. The municipality's blend of urban accessibility and natural beauty means you can often reach trailheads conveniently from metro stations.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Erandio?
A: Yes, Erandio is generally dog-friendly, especially in its numerous green spaces. Parque Bizkotxe is specifically noted for being pet-friendly, featuring a large fenced dog off-leash area, which can be a great starting point for a run with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control and check local regulations for specific areas.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I see while jogging in Erandio?
A: Erandio's routes offer various scenic views and landmarks. You can enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/569708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbao Estuary&lt;/a&gt;, the Arrontegi Zubia (bridge), and the wider Bilbao area. Some trails lead to summits like Monte Karabezu, providing panoramic vistas. Additionally, you might encounter historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4212898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de la Quadra&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the region's rich heritage.

Q: Are there any parks or green spaces suitable for running in Erandio?
A: Yes, Erandio boasts several parks and green spaces ideal for running. Santi Brouard Parkea, located behind the Erandio City Hall, offers a pleasant green space within the urban core. Parque Bizkotxe is another excellent option, known for its pet-friendly facilities. The Goiherri neighborhood also provides a rural character with ample natural areas to explore.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Erandio?
A: Runners frequently praise Erandio for its diverse landscapes, which seamlessly blend urban accessibility with natural beauty. The scenic riverwalks along the Nervión River and Bilbao Estuary are highly appreciated, as are the opportunities for more challenging runs in the hills. The variety of routes, from gentle paths to summit trails, ensures there's something for every ability level.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in Erandio?
A: Yes, Erandio offers routes suitable for longer runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31952859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Karabezu Summit – View from Monte Karabezu loop from Astrabudua&lt;/a&gt;, at 13.1 miles (21.1 km), is an excellent option for those seeking a longer, more enduring run through hilly terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Erandio?
A: Erandio's climate is generally mild, making it suitable for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summer can be warm, especially in direct sunlight, while winter might bring cooler, wetter conditions, though extreme cold is rare. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.
tips: Beautiful church with more than two centuries of history.
Very nice trail to run. Entertaining and with good views of the Bilbao estuary. In particular, the sunsets are spectacular.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Andra Mari Eleiza y Camino de las Tres Cruces jogging route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 10.3 kilometers long. Most joggers can expect to complete it in about 1 hour and 7 minutes, depending on their pace and fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of paved tracks, making it a very good path for running. While generally accessible, some sections may require sure footing.

faqs.question: What notable sights can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by the historic Church of the Assumption of Our Lady (Andra Mari Eleiza) and the scenic Path of the Three Crosses. The route also offers good views of the Bilbao estuary.

faqs.question: Is the Andra Mari Eleiza y Camino de las Tres Cruces trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this route as 'moderate'. While it's a great path for running, a good fitness level is recommended due to its length and some elevation gain. Beginners might find it challenging but achievable with breaks.

faqs.question: How can I access the trailhead, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The starting point in Erandio is easily accessible by public transport. While specific parking details aren't provided, Erandio generally offers public parking options. Consider using public transport for convenience.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash, especially in populated areas or near roads, and remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant weather for jogging in Erandio, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable then. Winter can be cool but often still suitable for outdoor activity.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to use the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog along the Andra Mari Eleiza y Camino de las Tres Cruces trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several local paths. You'll find yourself on sections of trails like Eskuinaldea - Uribe Kosta, Llona kalea, Beaskoetxe-Arriaga bidea, and Urdaneta kalea, among others, for short stretches.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Erandio, you'll find various amenities, including cafes and restrooms, within the town itself. It's advisable to use facilities before or after your run.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers good views of the Bilbao estuary, providing a scenic backdrop to your run. You'll also experience the local landscape around Erandio, which includes some geological interest with volcanic rock formations in the broader area.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Our Lady (Andra Mari Eleiza)
